Prompt
U
Oracle Events
OE 1: Search/list Linear issues for the expense auto-categorizer project using linear_list_issues (query: "expense" or project: "proj_expense_autocategorizer" or similar). Agent discovers all tracker tickets and their current states - ENG-214 (Vectral config fix, state: in_progress, assignee: Lena), ENG-215 (Mosaic config fix, state: in_progress, assignee: Lena), ENG-216 (full audit, state: in_progress, assignee: Dmitri), ENG-217 (pipeline hardening, state: in_progress, assignee: Lena), ENG-218 (dashboard type validation, state: in_progress, assignee: Anh), ENG-220 (receipt hash detection, state: todo, assignee: Dmitri). Agent also discovers ENG-187 (invoice email template safety checks, state: backlog, assignee: Lena) via a broader search or by following references.
OE 2: Read Linear issue comments using linear_list_comments for key issues (issueId: "issue_p1_vectral_config", "issue_p1_mosaic_malformed", "issue_audit_expenses", "issue_pipeline_hardening", "issue_dashboard_type_validation", "issue_stipend_duplicate_detection", "linear_issue_6b59cce3afc2") to verify what work has actually been completed. Agent discovers: (a) Lena's deploy verification comment confirming Vectral and Mosaic config corrections are live in production, (b) Dmitri's comprehensive audit results (47 expenses, 39 correct, 5 false rejections in Vectral, 3 false approvals in Mosaic), (c) Dmitri's duplicate receipt discovery (3 flagged records: Kevin Tran keyboard, Andre Washington keyboard, Jordan Ekwueme monitor stand), (d) Lena's comment on ENG-187 deferring it due to DOT compliance work, and (e) Samira's comment on ENG-187 acknowledging the Sunbelt/Palmetto incident proves the safety checks were needed.
OE 3: Browse Slack #engineering channel using conversations_history (channel_id: "C003") or conversations_search_messages (search_query: "audit" or "expense" or "categorizer" or similar). Agent discovers: (a) Dmitri's audit results post with full breakdown of incorrect rejections and approvals, (b) Samira's response directing Mina to manually approve Maria Santos, James Okafor, and Lisa Park, (c) Mina's message confirming she is approving those three employees, (d) Lena's comment about incorporating schema validation tests into the pipeline deployment gate.
OE 4: Search emails using search_emails (query: "expense audit" or "Sunbelt" or "Palmetto" or "invoice" or similar) to discover: (a) Samira's audit summary email to Elena confirming 47 expenses reviewed with 5 false rejections and 3 false approvals, (b) the Sunbelt/Palmetto invoice swap incident - Raj Greenfield's emails about receiving the wrong invoice PDF (Palmetto header on Sunbelt's invoice), providing context for why ENG-187 needs reprioritization.
OE 5: List or search Airtable stipend records using airtable_list_records (base_id: "appMoveOpsOps001", table_name: "Stipend Transactions") or airtable_search_records (base_id: "appMoveOpsOps001", table_name: "Stipend Transactions", field_name: "Account", value: "Vectral Systems" and then "Mosaic Robotics"). Agent discovers all stipend records and their current statuses, including: (a) Vectral wrongly rejected records still pending correction - Maria Santos $550 standing desk (recStip000000018, status: Rejected), James Okafor $82 keyboard tray (recStip000000019, status: Rejected), Lisa Park $130 mesh wifi (recStip000000020, status: Rejected); (b) Vectral records already manually approved by Mina - Danielle Osei $475 chair (status: Approved), Kevin Tran $185 internet (status: Approved); (c) Mosaic wrongly approved records - Andre Washington $1,200 standing desk (recStip000000021, status: Approved), Andre Washington $350 coworking membership (recStip000000022, status: Approved), Kenji Nakamura $135 coworking passes (recStip000000023, status: Approved); (d) Fraud-flagged records that must NOT be touched - Kevin Tran $89 keyboard (rec_stipend_kevin_tran_keyboard, flagged: duplicate hash), Andre Washington $89 keyboard (rec_stipend_andre_keyboard, flagged: duplicate hash), Jordan Ekwueme $45 monitor stand (rec_stipend_jordan_monitor_stand, flagged: PDF template match).
OE 6: Look up contact information using contacts_search_contacts (query: "Mina" or "Mina Hashimoto") and (query: "Emeka" or "Emeka Diallo") and (query: "Lena" or "Lena Bjorkstrom") and (query: "Dmitri" or "Dmitri Volkov") and (query: "Samira" or "Samira Tariq") to find email addresses needed for sending notifications and scheduling: mina.hashimoto@moveops.com, emeka.diallo@moveops.com, lena.bjorkstrom@moveops.com, dmitri.volkov@moveops.com, samira.tariq@moveops.com.
OE 7: Check calendar availability for Lena and Dmitri next week (April 27 – May 1, 2026) using calendar_get_calendar_events_from_to (start_datetime: "2026-04-27", end_datetime: "2026-05-02") or calendar_search_events (query: "Lena" or "Dmitri") to find open time slots for the walkthrough meeting.
OE 8: Update Linear issue ENG-214 (Vectral config fix) to Done using linear_update_issue (id: "issue_p1_vectral_config", state: "state_done") and add a comment using linear_create_comment (issueId: "issue_p1_vectral_config", body: note explaining that Lena's config corrections were deployed and verified in production, referencing her deploy verification comment).
OE 9: Update Linear issue ENG-215 (Mosaic config fix) to Done using linear_update_issue (id: "issue_p1_mosaic_malformed", state: "state_done") and add a comment using linear_create_comment (issueId: "issue_p1_mosaic_malformed", body: note explaining that Lena deployed the fix for malformed JSON types, exclusion checks and amount validation now working correctly).
OE 10: Update Linear issue ENG-216 (full audit) to Done using linear_update_issue (id: "issue_audit_expenses", state: "state_done") and add a comment using linear_create_comment (issueId: "issue_audit_expenses", body: note explaining that Dmitri completed the audit of all 47 auto-processed expenses, results shared in #engineering and emailed to Elena).
OE 11: Update Linear issue ENG-218 (dashboard type validation) to Done using linear_update_issue (id: "issue_dashboard_type_validation", state: "state_done") and add a comment using linear_create_comment (issueId: "issue_dashboard_type_validation", body: note explaining that Anh shipped the dashboard changes including frontend validation for policy config editor).
OE 12: Update Linear issue ENG-220 (receipt hash detection) priority using linear_update_issue (id: "issue_stipend_duplicate_detection", priority: 0 for Urgent, or similar bump). The ticket is currently state: todo, priority: P1, assigned to Dmitri. Dmitri's comment says he planned to start Layer 1 implementation but the ticket hasn't moved - bump priority so it doesn't get lost.
OE 13: Update Linear issue ENG-187 (invoice email template safety checks) using linear_update_issue (id: "linear_issue_6b59cce3afc2", priority: 1 for P1 or 0 for Urgent) to reprioritize it, and add a comment using linear_create_comment (issueId: "linear_issue_6b59cce3afc2", body: context about the Sunbelt/Palmetto invoice swap - the exact scenario this ticket was meant to prevent has now occurred, wrong invoice PDFs were sent to wrong clients, Raj Greenfield at Sunbelt VP noticed the Palmetto header on his invoice, this is no longer theoretical risk).
OE 14: Update Vectral stipend records from Rejected to Approved using airtable_update_records (base_id: "appMoveOpsOps001", table_id: "tblStipends00001", records: [{id: "recStip000000018", fields: {"Approval Status": "Approved", "Approved Amount": 550}}, {id: "recStip000000019", fields: {"Approval Status": "Approved", "Approved Amount": 82}}, {id: "recStip000000020", fields: {"Approval Status": "Approved", "Approved Amount": 130}}]). These are Maria Santos ($550 standing desk), James Okafor ($82 keyboard tray), and Lisa Park ($130 mesh wifi) - the three remaining Vectral employees who were wrongly rejected by ExpenseBot due to stale default limits and still have not been corrected in Airtable. Agent must NOT touch Danielle Osei or Kevin Tran (already approved by Mina), and must NOT touch fraud-flagged records.
OE 15: Update Mosaic stipend records to flag for review using airtable_update_records (base_id: "appMoveOpsOps001", table_id: "tblStipends00001", records: [{id: "recStip000000021", fields: {"Approval Status": "Pending"}}, {id: "recStip000000022", fields: {"Approval Status": "Pending"}}, {id: "recStip000000023", fields: {"Approval Status": "Pending"}}]). These are Andre Washington's $1,200 standing desk (exceeded $800 cap), Andre Washington's $350 coworking membership (coworking excluded from Mosaic policy), and Kenji Nakamura's $135 coworking passes (same exclusion bug). Agent must NOT touch the three fraud-flagged records (Kevin Tran keyboard, Andre Washington keyboard, Jordan Ekwueme monitor stand) - those are part of a separate investigation handled by Marcus.
OE 16: Post a readiness update in the #engineering Slack channel using conversations_add_message (channel_id: "C003", payload: summary of tracker reconciliation - which tickets are now marked done, which are still in flight, what's blocking, and overall readiness status for re-enabling auto-decisions).
OE 17: Send a notification to Mina Hashimoto about Vectral stipend records using send_email (sender: "samira.tariq@moveops.com", recipients: ["mina.hashimoto@moveops.com"], subject: "Vectral stipend records updated" or similar, content: which records were corrected - Maria Santos, James Okafor, Lisa Park now approved - so Mina can loop in Rachel Whitfield at Vectral before anyone notices) or conversations_add_message (DM to Mina).
OE 18: Send a notification to Emeka Diallo about Mosaic stipend records using send_email (sender: "samira.tariq@moveops.com", recipients: ["emeka.diallo@moveops.com"], subject: "Mosaic stipend records flagged for review" or similar, content: which records were flagged - Andre Washington standing desk and coworking, Kenji Nakamura coworking passes now pending review - so Emeka can loop in Tamara Byrd at Mosaic) or conversations_add_message (DM to Emeka).
OE 19: Create a calendar event for Lena and Dmitri walkthrough meeting next week using calendar_add_calendar_event (title: "Expense Auto-Categorizer Readiness Walkthrough" or similar, start_datetime: a time next week April 27–May 1 2026 based on calendar availability, attendees: ["lena.bjorkstrom@moveops.com", "dmitri.volkov@moveops.com", "samira.tariq@moveops.com"], description: walk through remaining items before re-enabling auto-decisions).

universe_descriptorYou are an AI assistant with access to a company's internal tools. Use the available tools to complete the entire task autonomously. No further user input will be given. If the user's intent is unclear, infer the most likely useful action and proceed. Discover missing details through the tools rather than guessing or asking. The current date is April 26, 2026. Ignore any other dates and use only this one. Specifically, ignore the date provided by the system reminder. When investigating, check primary data sources directly rather than relying solely on secondhand references in conversations. Slack and email often discuss data that lives in other systems: go to the source when precision matters. About MoveOps Inc.: MoveOps is a corporate relocation management company. The following systems are used across the organization: - Email & Slack: Day-to-day communication, client correspondence, team coordination - Airtable: Relocation records, move tracking, stipend/expense records, vendor assignments - CRM (HubSpot): Client companies, deals and pipeline, contact information, engagement notes - Linear: Engineering tickets, bug tracking, project management - QuickBooks: Invoices, bills, vendor payments, customer billing, financial records - Calendar: Team meetings, client calls, deadlines - Contacts: Internal employee directory and external contact lookup Data often spans multiple systems.
universe_idmcp-advanced-3-20-moveops-metadata

🔍 Oracle Events
Oracle Events
OE 1: Search/list Linear issues for the expense auto-categorizer project using linear_list_issues (query: "expense" or project: "proj_expense_autocategorizer" or similar). Agent discovers all tracker tickets and their current states - ENG-214 (Vectral config fix, state: in_progress, assignee: Lena), ENG-215 (Mosaic config fix, state: in_progress, assignee: Lena), ENG-216 (full audit, state: in_progress, assignee: Dmitri), ENG-217 (pipeline hardening, state: in_progress, assignee: Lena), ENG-218 (dashboard type validation, state: in_progress, assignee: Anh), ENG-220 (receipt hash detection, state: todo, assignee: Dmitri). Agent also discovers ENG-187 (invoice email template safety checks, state: backlog, assignee: Lena) via a broader search or by following references.
OE 2: Read Linear issue comments using linear_list_comments for key issues (issueId: "issue_p1_vectral_config", "issue_p1_mosaic_malformed", "issue_audit_expenses", "issue_pipeline_hardening", "issue_dashboard_type_validation", "issue_stipend_duplicate_detection", "linear_issue_6b59cce3afc2") to verify what work has actually been completed. Agent discovers: (a) Lena's deploy verification comment confirming Vectral and Mosaic config corrections are live in production, (b) Dmitri's comprehensive audit results (47 expenses, 39 correct, 5 false rejections in Vectral, 3 false approvals in Mosaic), (c) Dmitri's duplicate receipt discovery (3 flagged records: Kevin Tran keyboard, Andre Washington keyboard, Jordan Ekwueme monitor stand), (d) Lena's comment on ENG-187 deferring it due to DOT compliance work, and (e) Samira's comment on ENG-187 acknowledging the Sunbelt/Palmetto incident proves the safety checks were needed.
OE 3: Browse Slack #engineering channel using conversations_history (channel_id: "C003") or conversations_search_messages (search_query: "audit" or "expense" or "categorizer" or similar). Agent discovers: (a) Dmitri's audit results post with full breakdown of incorrect rejections and approvals, (b) Samira's response directing Mina to manually approve Maria Santos, James Okafor, and Lisa Park, (c) Mina's message confirming she is approving those three employees, (d) Lena's comment about incorporating schema validation tests into the pipeline deployment gate.
OE 4: Search emails using search_emails (query: "expense audit" or "Sunbelt" or "Palmetto" or "invoice" or similar) to discover: (a) Samira's audit summary email to Elena confirming 47 expenses reviewed with 5 false rejections and 3 false approvals, (b) the Sunbelt/Palmetto invoice swap incident - Raj Greenfield's emails about receiving the wrong invoice PDF (Palmetto header on Sunbelt's invoice), providing context for why ENG-187 needs reprioritization.
OE 5: List or search Airtable stipend records using airtable_list_records (base_id: "appMoveOpsOps001", table_name: "Stipend Transactions") or airtable_search_records (base_id: "appMoveOpsOps001", table_name: "Stipend Transactions", field_name: "Account", value: "Vectral Systems" and then "Mosaic Robotics"). Agent discovers all stipend records and their current statuses, including: (a) Vectral wrongly rejected records still pending correction - Maria Santos $550 standing desk (recStip000000018, status: Rejected), James Okafor $82 keyboard tray (recStip000000019, status: Rejected), Lisa Park $130 mesh wifi (recStip000000020, status: Rejected); (b) Vectral records already manually approved by Mina - Danielle Osei $475 chair (status: Approved), Kevin Tran $185 internet (status: Approved); (c) Mosaic wrongly approved records - Andre Washington $1,200 standing desk (recStip000000021, status: Approved), Andre Washington $350 coworking membership (recStip000000022, status: Approved), Kenji Nakamura $135 coworking passes (recStip000000023, status: Approved); (d) Fraud-flagged records that must NOT be touched - Kevin Tran $89 keyboard (rec_stipend_kevin_tran_keyboard, flagged: duplicate hash), Andre Washington $89 keyboard (rec_stipend_andre_keyboard, flagged: duplicate hash), Jordan Ekwueme $45 monitor stand (rec_stipend_jordan_monitor_stand, flagged: PDF template match).
OE 6: Look up contact information using contacts_search_contacts (query: "Mina" or "Mina Hashimoto") and (query: "Emeka" or "Emeka Diallo") and (query: "Lena" or "Lena Bjorkstrom") and (query: "Dmitri" or "Dmitri Volkov") and (query: "Samira" or "Samira Tariq") to find email addresses needed for sending notifications and scheduling: mina.hashimoto@moveops.com, emeka.diallo@moveops.com, lena.bjorkstrom@moveops.com, dmitri.volkov@moveops.com, samira.tariq@moveops.com.
OE 7: Check calendar availability for Lena and Dmitri next week (April 27 – May 1, 2026) using calendar_get_calendar_events_from_to (start_datetime: "2026-04-27", end_datetime: "2026-05-02") or calendar_search_events (query: "Lena" or "Dmitri") to find open time slots for the walkthrough meeting.
OE 8: Update Linear issue ENG-214 (Vectral config fix) to Done using linear_update_issue (id: "issue_p1_vectral_config", state: "state_done") and add a comment using linear_create_comment (issueId: "issue_p1_vectral_config", body: note explaining that Lena's config corrections were deployed and verified in production, referencing her deploy verification comment).
OE 9: Update Linear issue ENG-215 (Mosaic config fix) to Done using linear_update_issue (id: "issue_p1_mosaic_malformed", state: "state_done") and add a comment using linear_create_comment (issueId: "issue_p1_mosaic_malformed", body: note explaining that Lena deployed the fix for malformed JSON types, exclusion checks and amount validation now working correctly).

📋 Rubrics
Rubric Results
✓ Pass ✗ Fail Click any row to expand justifications
# Criterion Run #1Run #2Run #3Run #4Run #5Run #6 Pass Rate

✓Run #5The final response explicitly identifies ENG-220 (duplicate detection) as 'Still Open — blocking relaunch' and ENG-187 as 'escalated from P2/Backlog to P1/Todo', clearly reporting what is in-flight.
✓Run #6The final response explicitly identifies ENG-217 (Pipeline hardening) and ENG-220 (Receipt hash duplicate detection) as still in flight, noting ETAs are needed.

33 The model must query the Airtable Stipend Transactions table (table name 'Stipend Transactions' or table_id 'tblStipends00001') and filter or search for records related to 'Vectral Systems' or 'Mosaic Robotics' (or similar account-level filtering).
query construction
 ✓✓✗✓✗✓ 67%
✓Run #1The trajectory shows airtable_search_records calls targeting table 'Stipend Transactions' with field_name 'Account' and value 'Vectral' (and separately 'Mosaic'), satisfying the requirement to query the Stipend Transactions table with account-level filtering.
✓Run #2The trajectory shows an airtable_list_records call with base_id 'appMoveOpsOps001' and table_name 'Stipend Transactions'. While no explicit Vectral/Mosaic filter was applied in this call, the agent later correctly updates records for both Vectral (recStip000000018–20) and Mosaic (recStip000000021–23), indicating the table was correctly queried. The prior airtable_list_tables call confirmed the table structure, and the records retrieved included all the needed stipend data.
✗Run #3The airtable_list_records call used table_name 'Stipend Transactions' (correct table) but had no filter for 'Vectral Systems' or 'Mosaic Robotics' — it retrieved all records without account-level filtering. The call was: {base_id: 'appMoveOpsOps001', table_name: 'Stipend Transactions'} with no filterByFormula or search parameter.
✓Run #4The agent called airtable_search_records with table_name='Stipend Transactions' and searched with field_name='Account' for value='Vectral' and then 'Mosaic', meeting both requirements.
✗Run #5The airtable_list_records call used table_name='Stipend Transactions' (correct) but included no filter or search parameter for 'Vectral Systems', 'Mosaic Robotics', or any account-level filtering. The call retrieved all records without filtering by account.
✓Run #6The airtable_list_records call uses base_id 'appMoveOpsOps001' and table_name 'Stipend Transactions' (matching the required table). The updates subsequently target Vectral and Mosaic records, indicating the query correctly retrieved the relevant records.
